concerns.text: FR-007 explicitly mandates a 'one-sample t-test' to determine statistical significance. Task T023 replaces this with 'Bootstrap CI', citing a plan.md note that claims t-test is invalid. This is a direct semantic weakening of the functional requirement. The plan's Complexity Tracking contradicts the spec's FR-007; the task must follow the spec's explicit requirement (t-test) or the spec must be updated to reflect the Bootstrap CI change before tasks are generated.

FR-004 defines Answer Correctness as 'Exact Match OR semantic similarity >= 0.85'. Task T022 (and T008) implements it as 'Exact Match OR (Semantic Similarity >= 0.85 AND Ground Truth Answer present in retrieved context)'. This adds an unrequested 'context present' constraint to the answer correctness logic, altering the mathematical definition of the metric defined in the spec. This violates the constraint that tasks must not silently relax or alter FR definitions.

The plan explicitly lists 'CPU-Only Constraints' (7GB RAM, no GPU) and rejects large models. Task T028 suggests implementing 'llava-1.5-7b' (even 4-bit quantized) for the Visual-Only control. Running a 7B parameter model on a 7GB CPU-only runner is highly likely to exceed memory constraints or crash, violating the plan's own hard constraints. This task introduces a high risk of failing the SC-004 runtime/memory success criteria.

The spec's Assumptions state 'The Phi-mini model (quantized to 4-bit) fits within the 7 GB RAM constraint'. Task T014 explicitly targets '4-bit quantized' Phi-3-mini. While this aligns with the assumption, the task description adds 'Ensure memory usage < 7GB' as a deliverable. However, the spec's FR-002 and SC-004 mandate the system MUST complete within 6 hours and 7GB RAM. The task does not explicitly address the 'held-out test set' constraint from FR-002 in its description, potentially allowing a task that runs on the full dataset (violating zero-shot generalization requirements) if not strictly scoped.
